source_jobs.title: UI Designer
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist UI Designer

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote

Compensation:

 - Competitive salary paid in USD

 - Negotiable range based on experience and location



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.



About The Role

You'll own the visual quality of Colonist's UI. That means translating game mechanics into beautiful, intuitive interfaces, and making every button, animation, and screen feel like it belongs in a world players want to stay in. You'll work closely with developers and product to ship work that reaches millions of players.

This isn't a support role. We want someone who pushes quality forward independently and treats the product like their own.



What You'll Do

 - Create high-quality UI designs that set a new standard for creativity and usability

 - Create animations and motion that make interactions feel alive and intentional

 - Collaborate with developers and QA to ensure designs ship as intended

 - Identify and solve visual problems without waiting to be asked



Requirements

 - AI first-mindset. We see AI fluency as a real multiplier

 - Strong portfolio showing game UI, interaction design, or animation work (link yours when applying)

 - Proficiency in Figma or equivalent tools

 - Solid grasp of animation principles and motion design

 - Understanding of game mechanics and how UI serves gameplay

 - Clear, structured written communication; this is a remote, async team
   
   

Nice to Have

 - Basic scripting for prototyping (JavaScript, C#, or AI-assisted tooling)

 - Game sound design experience
   
   

Our Values at Colonist

We strongly adhere to these values in our team - if these resonate, then you’ll love working here!

Dependability

We're self-driven players who deliver without needing supervision. When we commit to something, we make it happen.

Ownership

We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. Each of us acts like we own the company, because through our stock options, we do.

Clear Communication

In our remote environment, written communication is key. We're direct, structured, and always keep our team in the loop.

Efficient Execution

We move fast and focus on impact. Instead of getting stuck on perfect solutions, we find practical ways forward and get things done.

Continuous Learning

We're curious problem solvers who love a challenge. We constantly seek new ideas, embrace feedback, and aren't afraid to change our minds when we're wrong.

Why Colonist

 - Your work ships to 3,6M+ yearly active players

 - Async culture; deep work, no performative meetings

 - Unlimited vacation, yearly team offsite, full work equipment

 - Budget to experiment with any AI tool you want

 - Stock options: you'll own a piece of what you're building

 - Access to all company metrics and internal discussions

source_jobs.title: Talent Pool
source_jobs.description:
Disclaimer: This talent pool is not tied to an active role. It exists for candidates interested in joining Colonist when a relevant opportunity opens up. Please note that we can't guarantee if or when we'll reach out.



Working Hours: We are asynchronous, work when you want

Location: Fully Remote

Eligible Locations: Anywhere



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 2,466 years playing 45 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.



What We're Looking For

Individuals with an unstoppable mentality who can thrive in a fast-paced environment. Working here won't be easy, it's not a 9-5 role in a big company. It comes with a lot of hard work and long hours. But with that, comes more impact, learnings, and growth than anywhere else. We want people who aren't just looking for another job but are excited about building something new, increasing their skills and taking on bigger projects. There will be 100 things to do at any point and you'll need to prioritize actions that will move the needle.

As a Colonist you should:

 - Be a logical and independent problem solver

 - A team player - you are adaptable, proactive and highly responsive

 - A gamer with strong judgment

 - Be relentless, driven & hands-on - you get things done despite limited resources or circumstances going against you
   
   

Our Values at Colonist

We strongly adhere to these values in our team - if these resonate, then you’ll love working here!

Dependability

We're self-driven players who deliver without needing supervision. When we commit to something, we make it happen.

Ownership

We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. Each of us acts like we own the company, because through our stock options, we do.

Clear Communication

In our remote environment, written communication is key. We're direct, structured, and always keep our team in the loop.

Efficient Execution

We move fast and focus on impact. Instead of getting stuck on perfect solutions, we find practical ways forward and get things done.

Continuous Learning

We're curious problem solvers who love a challenge. We constantly seek new ideas, embrace feedback, and aren't afraid to change our minds when we're wrong.



Benefits and Compensation

Compensation

 - Competitive salary paid in USD

 - Stock (own a piece of Colonist)

Benefits

 - Fully remote team spread across multiple time zones

 - Flexible hours, we work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

 - Unlimited vacation

 - Yearly offsite

 - Work equipment provided

Growth

 - Work directly impacts 5M+ yearly active players

 - Rapid learning opportunities

 - Work with exceptional colleagues on challenging projects

 - Freedom to identify and solve problems

 - Access to all company metrics and discussions

 - Expect deep work and autonomy with clear expectations and accountability

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist Product Engineer

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote

Compensation:

 - Competitive salary paid in USD

 - Negotiable range based on experience and location



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.



About The Role

As a Product Engineer at Colonist, your mission is to develop and enhance game modes and features to deliver excellent player experiences. You will oversee the full development of game interfaces, ensuring seamless UI/UX across platforms, high code quality, performance, and stability. Your technical skills and player-focused mindset will shape how 3,6 million players engage with our games each year.



What You’ll Do

 - Evolve our game architecture, ensuring cleaner code boundaries and better scalability

 - Architect multi-platform systems to enable consistent features

 - Scale the codebase to support multiple game titles, while maintaining performance and developer velocity

 - Own full-stack development, from backend logic to polished UI

 - Balance technical complexity and product value, helping prioritize work that brings the biggest impact to players

 - Review pull requests and mentor peers to ensure code quality, maintainability, and strong architectural foundations

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to turn concepts into highly engaging and intuitive player experiences

 - Identify and optimize performance bottlenecks, ensuring gameplay feels smooth even under heavy load

 - Continuously improve tools and workflows, leveraging automation, testing, and CI/CD for faster iteration

Requirements

 - Deep experience with modern full-stack development

 - AI first-mindset. We see AI fluency as a real multiplier

 - Strong grasp of Git, code reviews, and collaborative workflows

 - Proven ability to design and maintain scalable architectures

 - Skilled in React, TypeScript, CSS, and building high-performance UIs

 - Strong written communication, you explain complex ideas clearly in async settings

 - Experienced with relational databases and REST APIs

 - Passionate about board games, both as a player and a developer

Nice to Have

 - Knowledge of data analysis or SQL for player insights

 - Contributions to open-source or dev communities

Our Values at Colonist

We strongly adhere to these values in our team - if these resonate, then you’ll love working here!

Dependability

We're self-driven players who deliver without needing supervision. When we commit to something, we make it happen.

Ownership

We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. Each of us acts like we own the company, because through our stock options, we do.

Clear Communication

In our remote environment, written communication is key. We're direct, structured, and always keep our team in the loop.

Efficient Execution

We move fast and focus on impact. Instead of getting stuck on perfect solutions, we find practical ways forward and get things done.

Continuous Learning

We're curious problem solvers who love a challenge. We constantly seek new ideas, embrace feedback, and aren't afraid to change our minds when we're wrong.

Why Colonist

 - Your work ships to 3,6M+ yearly active players

 - Async culture; deep work, no performative meetings

 - Unlimited vacation, yearly team offsite, full work equipment

 - Budget to experiment with any AI tool you want

 - Stock options: you'll own a piece of what you're building

 - Access to all company metrics and internal discussions

source_jobs.title: Product Associate
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist Product Associate

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote

Compensation:

 - Competitive salary paid in USD

 - Negotiable range based on experience and location

About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.

About The Role

As a Product Associate, you will accelerate growth by driving player activation and retention through data-driven insights and a deep understanding of user behavior. You’ll help scale our platform by organizing research, identifying UX issues, designing and running experiments, and transforming complex inputs into clear, testable product initiatives for a game with over 5 million annual players.

What You’ll Do

 - Research and analyze competitive game modes and premium features to create product matrices and propose experiments

 - Identify and document UX issues through structured investigation and competitor benchmarking

 - Create mockups and low-fidelity designs to communicate experiments and UX improvements

 - Define success metrics, design and sequence experiments, and estimate impact

 - Oversee experiments end-to-end: prepare specs, coordinate design and development, QA builds, launch, analyze results, and produce actionable reports

 - Analyze funnel and experiment data to generate actionable insights

 - Coordinate cross-functionally to execute experiments and maintain quality

 - Conduct user testing and playtests to validate solutions and gather insights

 - Break down complex projects into clear, testable steps for async execution

 - Report results, trade-offs, and recommendations in structured written formats

Requirements

 - AI first-mindset. We see AI fluency as a real multiplier

 - Exceptional attention to detail and high standards for clarity and execution

 - Strong written communication for async collaboration; able to write clear specs, experiment docs, and impact reports

 - Skilled at turning research into structured, actionable outputs (matrices, experiment plans, reports)

 - Experience designing and running experiments, defining success metrics, and conducting post-mortems

 - Able to identify UX issues and collaborate with designers to iterate and validate solutions through testing

 - Proficient in data analysis, interpretation, and presenting findings

 - Strong product intuition, familiar with mobile UX constraints and product lifecycle

Nice to Have

 - SQL proficiency

 - Ability to read code for deeper collaboration with devs

 - Forum/community moderation or support experience

Our Values at Colonist

Dependability
We’re self-driven players who deliver without supervision. When we commit, we deliver.

Ownership
We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. We act like owners, because through stock options, we actually are.

Clear Communication
In a remote environment, written clarity matters. We are direct, structured, and keep each other informed.

Efficient Execution
We move fast and focus on impact. Practical solutions that ship are preferred over perfect solutions that don’t.

Continuous Learning
We’re curious problem solvers who seek feedback, iterate often, and change our minds when shown better evidence.

Why Colonist

 - Your work ships to 3,6M+ yearly active players

 - Async culture; deep work, no performative meetings

 - Unlimited vacation, yearly team offsite, full work equipment

 - Budget to experiment with any AI tool you want

 - Stock options: you'll own a piece of what you're building

 - Access to all company metrics and internal discussions

source_jobs.title: Product Developer Intern
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist Product Developer Intern

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.



About The Role

As a Product Developer Intern at Colonist, your mission is to work closely with our game team to solve complex product problems and build engaging game modes, ensuring seamless UI/UX across platforms. You'll learn how high-quality games and products are actually built and help shape a game that reaches millions of players.



What You’ll Do

 - Build and improve game UI and UX, with a strong focus on clarity, polish, and fun

 - Contribute to new game modes, features, and gameplay experiences

 - Turn product ideas into working features used by real players

 - Work across the stack as needed

 - Collaborate asynchronously with product teams

 - Learn how to ship, iterate, and improve features based on real player behavior

Requirements

 - AI first-mindset. We see AI fluency as a real multiplier

 - Currently enrolled in a college course, preferably software development related

 - Available for a mid to long-term commitment (this is not a summer-only internship)

 - You enjoy building projects from start to finish

 - You care about user experience, not just the code

 - Motivated by solving challenging problems

 - Interested in board games

Nice to Have

You don’t need to check all of these boxes.

 - JavaScript / TypeScript

 - Git and version control

 - Side projects, prototypes, or experiments (finished or unfinished)

Our Values at Colonist

source_jobs.title: Community Manager
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist Community Manager

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote

Compensation: $2,000 – $3,000 USD/month (based on experience and location)



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.



About The Role

As Community Manager at Colonist, you are the voice, pulse, and connector of a fast-growing global gaming community. Your mission is to build lasting relationships, keep the community thriving across Discord and beyond, run live tournaments, and turn passionate players into lifelong advocates. You won't just manage a community, you'll shape the culture of one of the most active board game platforms on the internet.



You Will

 - Own Discord: moderate channels daily, enforce community rules, manage bots and roles

 - Write and publish all community content (patch notes, newsletters, announcements) on-brand and on time

 - Run weekly tournaments and competitive seasons: scheduling, brackets, real-time moderation, results

 - Build direct relationships with top players, streamers, and creators

 - Report weekly on community sentiment: what players love, hate, and want, pulled from Discord, Reddit, and social

 - Create onboarding content for new players: welcome flows, beginner guides, FAQs

 - Manage the referral program: track metrics, improve mechanics, coordinate with marketing

 - Produce posts, graphics (Canva), and short-form videos (CapCut)

You Are

 - Experienced in building and managing communities

 - A gamer who plays board games and understands online communities first-hand

 - AI-first. We see AI fluency as a real multiplier

 - A Discord expert: bots, permissions, server structure, moderation

 - A strong English writer: clear, warm, consistent across every channel

 - Organised: you manage competing priorities without missing deadlines

 - Empathetic: you understand players deeply and resolve conflicts calmly

 - Data-literate: you track metrics and turn community signals into actionable reports

 - Self-directed: you work async and deliver without hand-holding

 - Available on weekends for tournaments and live events

Bonus Points

 - You know Catan or have played on Colonist

 - You speak Spanish, Portuguese, or German

 - You have video editing skills (CapCut or similar)

 - You've grown or managed a gaming community before

Our Values at Colonist

We strongly adhere to these values in our team - if these resonate, then you’ll love working here!

Dependability

We're self-driven players who deliver without needing supervision. When we commit to something, we make it happen.

Ownership

We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. Each of us acts like we own the company, because through our stock options, we do.

Clear Communication

In our remote environment, written communication is key. We're direct, structured, and always keep our team in the loop.

Efficient Execution

We move fast and focus on impact. Instead of getting stuck on perfect solutions, we find practical ways forward and get things done.

Continuous Learning

We're curious problem solvers who love a challenge. We constantly seek new ideas, embrace feedback, and aren't afraid to change our minds when we're wrong.

Why Colonist

 - Your work ships to 3,6M+ yearly active players

 - Async culture; deep work, no performative meetings

 - Unlimited vacation, yearly team offsite, full work equipment

 - Budget to experiment with any AI tool you want

 - Stock options: you'll own a piece of what you're building

 - Access to all company metrics and internal discussions

source_jobs.title: QA Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Wanted: Colonist QA Engineer

Working Hours: We work asynchronously and value outcomes over hours

Location: Fully Remote

Compensation: $1,500 – $3,000 USD/month (based on experience and location)



About Colonist

We're building the biggest digital board game platform on the internet. Last year, our players spent 3,037 years playing 60 million games on our Settlers of Catan alternative. We're a fully remote, asynchronous team spread across multiple continents, focused on crafting extremely polished digital board games that people truly love.

About The Role

You'll own product quality for a game played by millions, and you'll do it by shipping fixes, not just filing tickets. Most QA roles stop at "I found the problem." This one doesn't. Your job is to make sure the product experience feels right across web, mobile, and Discord, catching what's broken, what's confusing, and what shouldn't have shipped the way it did.



You Will

 - Fix quality issues directly in the codebase

 - Drive fixes for larger issues end-to-end

 - Run weekly QA sessions on dev and staging builds on a fixed release cycle, with post-release production checks

 - Test PRs in a fast pace - happy path, edge cases, cross-platform - and approve or request changes with clear reasoning

 - Monitor Discord bug channels, validate player reports, and convert confirmed issues into structured tickets

 - Catch things that aren't bugs but are wrong: bad UX, illogical behavior, things that shouldn't have shipped the way they did

 - Ensure every significant change is tested across web, mobile, and Discord

 - Find gaps in the QA process proactively - propose fixes, introduce tools, document and roll out improvements without waiting to be asked

You Are

 - Experienced with full-cycle QA: test planning, execution, reporting, and follow-through

 - Sharp on detail and strong on judgment, you know when something is a bug and when it's a product problem

 - Comfortable in the codebase, enough to open a PR for smaller fixes yourself without blocking on engineering

 - Comfortable across platforms: web, mobile, Discord, and tools like GitHub, Linear, and BrowserStack

 - A clear async communicator: your bug reports are structured, your feedback is direct, and nothing gets lost

 - AI-fluent: you actively use AI tools to work faster and smarter

 - Self-directed: you find problems, figure out solutions, and ship without hand-holding

 - Responsive: you treat broken production like a fire

Bonus Points

 - You've done QA on multiplayer or real-time games

 - You have automation skills (Selenium, Playwright, Cypress) or want to grow into automation

 - You play Colonist or board games in general

Our Values at Colonist

We strongly adhere to these values in our team; if these resonate, then you'll love working here!

Dependability
We're self-driven players who deliver without needing supervision. When we commit to something, we make it happen.

Ownership
We take full responsibility and go beyond our roles to improve Colonist. Each of us acts like we own the company, because through our stock options, we do.

Clear Communication
In our remote environment, written communication is key. We're direct, structured, and always keep our team in the loop.

Efficient Execution
We move fast and focus on impact. Instead of getting stuck on perfect solutions, we find practical ways forward and get things done.

Continuous Learning
We're curious problem solvers who love a challenge. We constantly seek new ideas, embrace feedback, and aren't afraid to change our minds when we're wrong.

Why Colonist

 - Your work ships to 3,6M+ yearly active players

 - Async culture; deep work, no performative meetings

 - Unlimited vacation, yearly team offsite, full work equipment

 - Budget to experiment with any AI tool you want

 - Stock options: you'll own a piece of what you're building

 - Access to all company metrics and internal discussions

notes: Digital board game platform with a focus on polished games, fully remote team, and asynchronous work style.
